author: @R3TURNZ3R0
text: Thanks for the quick response, but I guess I probably should have known it was magnet wire when you said "magnetostrictive" lol.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hiya mate, glad you like the channel and yes it is magnet wire. you ned to use it to get enough amp turn to keep it small and have a powerful enough electromagnet to make it work - but my guess is you know the reasons why lol

author: @R3TURNZ3R0
text:
Hi Rob,
I was just wondering if the wire in between the rubber pieces is magnet wire, it looks like it, but I just wanted to be sure. I also wanted to tell you that I absolutely love your channel, it's like my second home now :) thank you for sharing your knowledge!

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Mail me for a fuller discussion robertmurraysmith64@gmail.com

author: @TheBaconWizard
text: It's a starting point at least, thanks.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: You need to look up giant magnetostrictive materials of which terfenol-D is an example. The foam was kind of an idea that struck me. If I were to revisit the idea I would probably look at templating a nanostructure via a soluble crystal matrix doped with foam laden with magnetite with a particle size at or slightly larger than the domain size which escapes me at the moment but I seem to remember it was around 4nm size, but check this. Hope this helps.

author: @TheBaconWizard
text:
Rob, I realise this isn't where your main research and interest lies, but I would really appreciate it if you could point me in the right direction for that (the magnetite foam idea)
Google didn't get me very far. I'm not afraid of doing my own work, but a solid starting-point would really help me with a design I am working on (I won't be making money from this, btw, I'm a robotics hobbyist)

author: @ThinkingandTinkering

text:
Cheers Mate,
I have got a new camera and microphone so video and audio quality should be tons better from now on. I will try to make my descriptions better - but above all I am glad you like them.

author: @JrgMP1
text:
Dear Mr Murray-Smith 
First would like to thank you for your time on making all this videos. Second I'm quite amaze and amuse by the things you can make. Third keep making more videos. 

Pointers:
1- used the video description to provide information on the stuff you used/make so others can try to replicate them at home. Example: materials used, quantity/amount, maybe even links were to buy it/ get it. 
2- please, you need to get a better recording equipment, like a used quality cellphone.
